# Quick Paste: iOS Clipboard Manager

> Quick Paste is a free, privacy-focused clipboard manager app for iPhone and iPad. It saves text, images, and links beyond iOS's default single-item clipboard limit, giving users a searchable clipboard history with Face ID protection, a paste keyboard extension, and optional iCloud sync across iPhone and iPad.

Quick Paste (App Store ID: 6748644352) is developed by Codebuster Apps and targets iPhone and iPad users on iOS who need to manage multiple copied items, reuse repeated content, and paste efficiently across apps — with the option to sync clipboard history across devices via iCloud.

<!-- Last updated: June 2026 — reflects all blog posts currently published -->

## Product Details

- **Platform**: iOS (iPhone & iPad)
- **Price**: Free
- **App Store**: https://apps.apple.com/pk/app/copy-paste-keyboard/id6748644352
- **Developer**: Codebuster Apps
- **Website**: https://quickpaste.codebusterapps.com
- **Storage**: On-device by default; optional iCloud sync across iPhone and iPad
- **Security**: Face ID protection per clip
- **Account required**: No

## Core Features

- Clipboard history: saves multiple copied items (text, images, links) unlike the iOS default which stores only the last item
- iCloud Sync: optionally sync clipboard history across iPhone and iPad via iCloud — clips saved on one device are available on the other
- Keyboard extension: paste saved clips directly from the iOS keyboard without switching apps
- Action Extension: save any copied content via the iOS Share sheet
- Face ID lock: optional biometric protection for clipboard history
- On-device storage by default: no mandatory cloud sync, no account required, fully private
- Lightweight and free

## Use Cases

- Professionals reusing email templates, signatures, and standard replies across iPhone and iPad
- Students saving research links, citations, and notes while switching between devices and apps
- Content creators managing promo codes, affiliate links, and brand deal copy
- Developers storing code snippets and command strings accessible on all their Apple devices
- iPad users who copy content on iPhone and need it instantly available on their iPad
- Anyone who frequently transfers information between iOS apps or across devices

## How It Works

1. Enable the Quick Paste Action Extension via iOS Share sheet settings
2. Copy any text, image, or link — save it to Quick Paste via Share → Copy to Quick Paste
3. While typing in any app, long-press the language switch key on the iOS keyboard
4. Select Quick Paste, view saved clips, tap any item to paste instantly
5. Optionally enable iCloud Sync in settings to access your clipboard history across all your iPhone and iPad devices

## FAQs

**Does iPhone support clipboard history by default?**
No. iOS only stores the most recently copied item. Once you copy something new, the previous item is permanently lost. A clipboard manager app adds persistent clipboard history to iPhone.

**Does Quick Paste sync across iPhone and iPad?**
Yes. Quick Paste supports optional iCloud sync, so your clipboard history is available across all your iPhone and iPad devices. Sync is opt-in — data stays on-device by default if you prefer.

**Is clipboard data private in Quick Paste?**
Yes. By default, all data is stored on-device only. If iCloud Sync is enabled, data is synced securely through your personal iCloud account. Clipboard history can also be locked with Face ID.

**Can you recover previously copied text on iPhone?**
Not with the default iOS clipboard — once replaced, it is gone. Quick Paste solves this by saving a full clipboard history so nothing is ever lost.

**Does Quick Paste work across all iPhone apps?**
Saved clips can be pasted across supported apps using the keyboard extension. The app uses iOS-permitted methods and does not intercept system-level clipboard at the OS level.

**Is Quick Paste free?**
Yes. The app is free with no mandatory in-app purchases.

**What content types can Quick Paste save?**
Text, images, and links.

**Who is Quick Paste useful for?**
Students, professionals, writers, developers, content creators, and anyone who copies text, links, or images more than once a day on iPhone or iPad.
